documentation:
# PyQt-builder - the PyQt Build System

PyQt-builder is the PEP 517 compliant build system for PyQt and projects that
extend PyQt.  It extends the [SIP](https://pypi.org/project/sip/) build system
and uses Qt's `qmake` to perform the actual compilation and installation of
extension modules.

Projects that use PyQt-builder provide an appropriate `pyproject.toml` file and
an optional `project.py` script.  Any PEP 517 compliant frontend, for example
`build` or `pip` can then be used to build and install the project.


## Documentation

The documentation can be found at
[Read the Docs](https://PyQt-builder.readthedocs.io).


## License

PyQt-builder is licensed under the BSD 2 clause license.


## Installation

To install SIP, run:

    pip install sip


## Creating Packages for Distribution

Python sdists and wheels can be created with any standard Python build
frontend.

For example, using [build](https://pypi.org/project/build/) an sdist and wheel
will be created from a checkout in the current directory by running:

    python -m build --outdir .


## Building the Documentation

The documentation is built using [Sphinx](https://pypi.org/project/Sphinx/),
[myst_parser](https://pypi.org/project/myst-parser/) and the
[sphinx-rtd-theme](https://pypi.org/project/sphinx-rtd-theme/) theme.

Change to the `docs` directory of a checkout and run:

    make html

The HTML documentation can then be found in the `_build/html` subdirectory.

documentation:
Create a wheel containing the subset of a Qt installation required for
a particular PyQt package.

documentation: Bundle a Qt installation with a PyQt wheel.

modules.name: pyqtbuild._version
documentation: don't change, don't track in version control

classes.name: pyqtbuild.installable.QmakeTargetInstallable
documentation:
An installable for the TARGET of a .pro file.  A TARGET is installed
automatically but this captures the information needed for the .dist-info
directory.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.project.PyQtProject.apply_nonuser_defaults
documentation: Set default values for non-user options that haven't been set yet.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.project.PyQtProject.apply_user_defaults
documentation: Set default values for user options that haven't been set yet.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.project.PyQtProject.get_platform_tag
documentation:
Return the platform tag to use in a wheel name.  This calls the
default implementation and applied the target Apple build type.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.project.PyQtProject.get_options
documentation: Return the list of configurable options.

documentation: Set default values for user options that haven't been set yet.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bindings.PyQtBindings.get_options
documentation: Return the list of configurable options.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bindings.PyQtBindings.handle_test_output
documentation:
Handle the output of any external test program and return True if
the bindings are buildable.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bindings.PyQtBindings.is_buildable
documentation: Return True of the bindings are buildable.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bindings.PyQtBindings._matching_files
documentation: Return a reproducable list of files that match a pattern.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bindings.PyQtBindings._update_builder_settings
documentation:
Update the builder settings with a list of modifications to a
value.
A base class for all PyQt-based bindings.

classes.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata.bundle
documentation: Bundle part of Qt as defined by the meta-data.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata.is_applicable
documentation:
Returns True if this meta-data is applicable for a particular Qt
version.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._bundle_nondebug
documentation: Bundle the non-debug contents of a directory.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._bundle_file
documentation:
Bundle a file (or directory) and return the name of the installed
file (or directory) or None if it was missing.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._create_qt_conf
documentation: Create a qt.conf file for an executable.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._get_macos_thin_arch
documentation:
Return the single macOS architecture, or None if the platform is
not macOS or is universal.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._get_qt_library_dir
documentation:
Return the name of the directory in the Qt installation containing
the libraries.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._get_qt_library_subdir
documentation:
Return the name of the sub-directory in the Qt installation
containing the libraries.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._impl_from_library
documentation: Return the architecture-specific name of a Qt library.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._is_debug
documentation: Return True if a name implies a debug version.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.bundle.qt_metadata.VersionedMetadata._is_platform
documentation: Return True if a metadata archtitecture matches a platform tag.

Encapsulate the meta-data for a set of bindings for a particular
version of Qt.

classes.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder.apply_user_defaults
documentation: Set default values for user options that haven't been set yet.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder.build_executable
documentation:
Build an executable from a BuildableExecutable object and return
the relative pathname of the executable.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder.get_options
documentation: Return the sequence of configurable options.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder.install_project
documentation: Install the project into a target directory.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder.qmake_quote
documentation: Return a path quoted for qmake if it contains spaces.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._find_exe
documentation: Find an executable, ie. the first on the path.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._find_make
documentation: Return the name of a valid make program.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._generate_module_pro_file
documentation:
Generate the .pro file for an extension module.  The list of
installed files is updated.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._get_qt_configuration
documentation: Run qmake to get the details of the Qt configuration.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._install
documentation:
Add the lines to install files to a .pro file and a list of all
installed files.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._is_exe
documentation: Return True if an executable exists.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._quote
documentation: Return a path with quotes added if it contains spaces.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._remove_file
documentation: Remove a file which may or may not exist.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._run_make
documentation:
Run make against a Makefile to create an executable.  Returns the
platform specific name of the executable, or None if an executable
wasn't created.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._run_project_make
documentation:
Run make on the project.  The Makefile must be in the current
directory.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._run_qmake
documentation:
Run qmake against a .pro file.  fatal is set if a qmake failure is
considered a fatal error, otherwise False is returned if qmake fails.
The current directory must contain the .pro file.

methods.name: pyqtbuild.builder.QmakeBuilder._update_pro_file
documentation: Update a .pro file from a buildable.
A project builder that uses qmake as the underlying build system.
